subject: Cloud Computing
title: Data Compression in the Cloud
description: A short lesson to help you apply Data Compression in the Cloud.

hook.text: A company stores 100 TB of logs. Compression shrinks that to 10 TB. Storage cost drops 90% instantly.

buildup.text: Compression reduces data size for storage and transfer. Algorithms like gzip, zstd, and Snappy trade CPU for space.

discovery.text: Columnar formats like Parquet and ORC compress data 10x better than raw CSV or JSON for analytical workloads.

twist.text: Compression saves storage costs but increases compute costs. Decompressing data on every read takes CPU time.

climax.text: The right compression format depends on whether you optimize for storage cost, query speed, or transfer time.

punchline.text: Compress to save storage. Decompress to use data. Balance both.

quiz.question: What is the trade-off of data compression?
options: It saves storage costs but increases CPU usage when data needs to be read
Compressed data cannot be queried or searched
Compression only works on text files, not binary data
